clinical_coherence.notes: Flawless clinical picture. 72M with AF not on anticoagulation, HTN, DM2, hyperlipidemia presenting with sudden left hemiplegia and global aphasia. Vitals show appropriate hypertension (185/100) and AF with RVR (HR 117). Neuro exam perfectly matches left MCA territory stroke: right gaze preference, left facial droop, left hemiplegia, left hemineglect, upgoing left plantar. NIHSS 18 appropriately scored.

tool_output_consistency.notes: All modalities concordant. MRI shows left MCA territory DWI restriction with M1 occlusion. ECG confirms AF with RVR. Labs show normal coagulation (confirming no anticoagulation), stress hyperglycemia, mildly elevated troponin. CTA confirms T-occlusion. Echo shows structural AF substrate. CT perfusion shows favorable mismatch. All findings support cardioembolic LVO stroke.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD I63.9 is the unspecified cerebral infarction code; I63.411 (cerebral infarction due to embolism of right MCA) would be more specific, though I63.9 is commonly used. Critical actions are comprehensive and clinically sound. Contraindicated actions are accurate. Key reasoning points are thorough. Differential is well-calibrated. Minor issue: key reasoning point mentions 'right hemiparesis' but patient has left hemiparesis; however HPI correctly describes left-sided weakness.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Perfectly calibrated as straightforward. Classic AF-related cardioembolic stroke with clear LKW, LVO on imaging, within thrombolysis window. No diagnostic ambiguity. Expected agent confidence of 0.9 is appropriate.

demographic_plausibility.notes: Highly realistic. 72-year-old Caucasian male, retired farmer, BMI 29.4, former smoker with 30 pack-year history, AF diagnosed 3 years ago with refusal of anticoagulation after a fall. Family history of MI and stroke. Comprehensive and believable social history including sedentary lifestyle limited by osteoarthritis.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case covering the full acute stroke management pathway: LKW determination, thrombolysis eligibility, LVO identification, bridging therapy, BP management, and secondary prevention with anticoagulation. Clinical pearls about time-is-brain and AF as commonest cardioembolic source are well-chosen.

issues_found: ICD code I63.9 is non-specific; more precise code available
Key reasoning incorrectly mentions 'right hemiparesis' when patient has left hemiparesis
strengths: Exceptionally detailed and realistic clinical narrative
Perfect concordance across all tool outputs
Comprehensive follow-up outputs covering full stroke workup
